query: NASA Human Lander Challenge 2026 winners announcement next generation systems

{"query":"NASA Human Lander Challenge 2026 winners announcement next generation systems","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1726,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"NASA announced California Polytechnic State University as the overall winner and recipient of the $10,000 top prize award for their Peltier-based Hydration Accumulation Terminal project.Jun 26, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/artemis-campaign-development-division/human-landing-system-program/nasa-announces-winners-for-2026-human-lander-challenge/#:~:text=NASA%20announced%20California%20Polytechnic%20State,based%20Hydration%20Accumulation%20Terminal%20project.","title":"NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge - NASA","content":"NASA has announced the top student-developed solutions for environmental control and life support systems in future crewed lunar landers from participants in the 2026 Human Lander Challenge.
The announcement marks the culmination of months of research by university teams working to advance technologies supporting the agency’s Artemis program that will return American astronauts to the Moon in 2028.
The challenge concluded June 25 following final technical presentations near NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama.
The annual competition concluded with an awards ceremony recognizing the top-performing teams out of the 12 finalists.
NASA announced California Polytechnic State University as the overall winner and recipient of the $10,000 top prize award for their Peltier-based Hydration Accumulation Terminal project.
Student solutions from the Human Lander Challenge could be incorporated into current work for the next-generation Artemis landers."},{"url":"https://hulc.nianet.org/","title":"HuLC – NASA's Human Lander Challenge","content":"NASA’s 2026 Human Lander Challenge (HuLC) invites collegiate student teams to develop innovative, systems-level solutions that improve critical aspects of Environmental Control Life Support System (ECLSS) performance.
# There will be a 2026 Human Lander Student Competition.Please check back in late August for Updates!"},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/stmd/prizes-challenges-crowdsourcing-program/center-of-excellence-for-collaborative-innovation-coeci/nasa-2026-human-lander-challenge/","title":"NASA 2026 Human Lander Challenge - NASA","content":"Sep 2, 2025 — Interdisciplinary teams are encouraged. Award: $126,000 in total prizes. Open Date: August 29, 2025. Notice of Intent Deadline: October 20, 2025.Read more
HuLC projects allow students to incorporate their coursework into real aerospace design concepts and work together in a team environment.
Award: $126,000 in total prizes"},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/artemis-campaign-development-division/human-landing-system-program/nasa-selects-finalist-teams-for-student-human-lander-challenge/","title":"NASA Selects Finalist Teams for Student Human Lander Challenge - NASA","content":"NASA has selected 12 student teams to develop solutions for storing and transferring the super-cold liquid propellants needed for future long-term exploration beyond Earth orbit.
The agency’s 2025 Human Lander Challenge is designed to inspire and engage the next generation of engineers and scientists as NASA and its partners prepare to send astronauts to the Moon through the Artemis campaign in preparation for future missions to Mars.
They will present their work to a panel of NASA and industry judges at the 2025 Human Lander Competition Forum in Huntsville, Alabama, near NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center, in June 2025.
The top three placing teams will share a total prize purse of $18,000.
NASA’s Human Lander Challenge is sponsored by the agency’s Human Landing System Program within the Exploration Systems Development Mission Directorate and managed by the National Institute of Aerospace.
For more information on NASA’s 2025 Human Lander Challenge, including team progress, visit the challenge website."},{"url":"https://ceng.calpoly.edu/connection/2026/07/cal-poly-team-wins-nasa-human-lander-challenge-with-cold-water-system-for-space-missions/","title":"Cal Poly Team Wins NASA Human Lander Challenge With ...","date":"7 days ago","content":"7 days ago — The mechanical engineering senior project team won the $10,000 top prize for a cold-water system designed to support future lunar and Mars ..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"NASA Human Lander Challenge 2026 winners announcement next generation systems","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://hulc.nianet.org","title":"HuLC – NASA's Human Lander Challenge","content":"NASA's Human Lander Challenge (HuLC). NASA's Human Lander Challenge. # NASA’s 2026 HUMAN LANDER CHALLENGE FINALISTS ANNOUNCED! **University of California, Davis,**“Evaporator-Based Potable Water Dispenser (EBPWD) for Long-Duration Environmental Control and Life Support Systems (ECLSS)”. NASA’s 2026 Human Lander Challenge (HuLC) invites collegiate student teams to develop innovative, systems-level solutions that improve critical aspects of Environmental Control Life Support System (ECLSS) performance. After graduating from Washington University in St. Louis with her BSME (Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering), BettyLynn worked at Johnson Space Center as a contractor in various roles including ETHOS (Environmental and THermal Operating Systems) Flight Control Instructor, ISS (International Space Station) ECLS (Environmental Control and Life Support) Sustaining Engineer, and ISS ECLS Sustaining Air Revitalization Subsystem Manager. **Zach Bryant** is one of the Environmental Control and Life Support System (ECLSS) Assistant Chief Engineers at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center (MSFC) in Huntsville, AL, where he helps lead the development and integration of life support systems for future human exploration missions.","score":0.8989177,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nasa.gov/directorates/esdmd/artemis-campaign-development-division/human-landing-system-program/nasa-announces-winners-for-2026-human-lander-challenge","title":"NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander ...","content":"Jun 26, 2026 — NASA announced California Polytechnic State University as the overall winner and recipient of the $10,000 top prize award for their Peltier-","score":0.82694983,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/eraudb/posts/were-pleased-to-announce-that-two-student-teams-from-each-of-our-residential-cam/1460824282752924","title":"We're pleased to announce that two student teams from each of our residential campuses were selected to compete in NASA's 2026 Human Lander Challenge (HuLC). Here, they presented their projects to a panel of NASA engineers and industry experts at a final forum in Huntsville, Alabama. While the Embry-Riddle Daytona Beach team finished in third place, we still value the hard work dedicated to their research!
